text: A microcontroller is like a little boss (computer) on a chip that is connected to positive and negative (power) and uses its fingers (pins) to switch 3 V on or off (HIGH/LOW), precisely controlled by a program (e.g., Blockly). So you can remember: the boss controls the fingers with power, and the program tells them when to switch on or off!

text:
A microcontroller, small and fine,  
controls electricity, that's how it has to be.  
Firmly connected with plus and minus,  
it lets pins explore in sync.  
HIGH and LOW, that's its game,  
programmable – that's its goal!
